description: F&B brands feature limited-time weekly menu items with specific date ranges and product highlights to create urgency and repeat engagement cycles.
structural_recipe: Post feature new or returning menu items with clear date windows (e.g., 'Jan 26-31'). Use high-energy urgency markers (emoji alerts like 🚨). Include rich product descriptions highlighting key ingredients and flavor profiles. Structure caption as announcement of returning favorite + new item introduction. Tag dates prominently to establish a predictable weekly rhythm.
why_it_works: Saudis respond to scarcity and limited-time offers that create FOMO. Weekly rotation establishes habit-forming content cadence encouraging followers to check back regularly. The combination of nostalgia (returning favorites) and novelty (new items) satisfies desire for both consistency and discovery.
transplantation_caveats: Verify actual product availability during stated date ranges to maintain trust
Adapt urgency messaging tone to align with brand voice and audience expectations
Consider regional/religious calendar events that may affect menu rotations
